item.title: The Building Of The Genius Savant
item.description: To achieve true autonomy and bypass exploitative societal structures, individuals must master AI through daily practice and the development of modular, agent-based programming systems.

item.title: The Age Of Code
item.description: The author demonstrates how AI can be used to rapidly develop complex software ecosystems, arguing that the technology empowers individual developers to achieve massive productivity gains that traditional corporate metrics fail to capture.

item.title: Miniature Autonomous Companies
item.description: The author proposes building "autonomous companies" managed by AI agents that sell UI templates as foundational blueprints for AI-driven application development, focusing on the practice of entrepreneurship rather than immediate profit.

item.title: Use Big Ideas: Speak To Your Programming Artificial Intelligence By Subtle Analogy
item.description: The author suggests that instead of using technical, variable-heavy programming instructions, one can more effectively guide AI by using real-world metaphors and structured XML blueprints to manage complex application designs.

item.title: Programming Language Design: Syntactic Sugar Over Data Streams For Ladies
item.description: The author proposes transitioning from traditional line-by-line programming to a reactive, stream-based paradigm that treats data as continuous pipelines to better manage the asynchronous nature of modern information flows.

item.title: XML For Girls Learning Programming
item.description: The post explores the potential of using AI to leverage XML's structural self-similarity to design complex, programmable architectures and formal language specifications.

item.title: Fake Education Is A Dead End
item.description: The text advocates for reclaiming one's potential through self-directed learning and the use of AI in programming to build independent, automated systems while escaping the restrictive stresses of traditional education and societal exploitation.

item.title: The Year Of The New Design Portfolio
item.description: I’ve been experimenting with AI‑assisted web design—from generating style guides and Bootstrap themes to automating Docker setups and visual programming—while building a self‑maintaining product ecosystem that blends creative UI components, symbolic coding, and future autonomous agents.

item.title: Woodland Adventures Are Not Scary
item.description: The post reflects on various personal encounters and observations of nature's unpredictable, strange, and beautiful elements, ranging from mysterious fox cries and flash floods to the well-behaved raccoons of the Nordhouse Dunes.

item.title: The Autonomous Agent Afternoon
item.description: The author describes their experience deploying an autonomous AI agent to independently implement software improvements and highlights the agent's potential for continuous self-improvement and learning their personal coding style.

item.title: Bodybuilding Is Easy; But, Unbelievable
item.description: The author contends that true bodybuilding is achieved through rhythmic, low‑weight eventually-heavier dumbbell routines combined with dance‑like motions, favoring gradual muscle adaptation over a year rather than heavy lifting which can only lead to a disastrous plateau.

item.title: Application Architecture For Young Ladies
item.description: A programmer encourages use of Bootstrap starter template to build a reactive web app whose structure is described by a single XML map, employing signals, combineLatest, and scoped variables to manage state transitions (rooms or states) that trigger actions on enter/exit, while the AI holds the power to rebuild the next application version at any time based on the XML blueprint.

item.title: Aipocalypse Meow: The Prices Went Up, And Here Is What You Need
item.description: The post explains how to set up an open‑source AI agent that uses a local AI model in part for command‑based programming and symbolic AI actions, making powerful but affordable AI accessible without heavy memory or external services.

item.title: A Cry for the Abolition of Poverty
item.description: The essay argues that contemporary poverty operates as an engineered form of modern slavery—systematically reducing people’s lives to raw labor and forcing teachers, children and workers into perpetual production—and calls for a renewed abolitionist effort to dismantle this machinery so all can truly flourish.

item.title: What Are Autonomous Artificial Intelligence Agents? A Very Simple Explanation
item.description: From simple autocomplete prompts to self‑looping coding agents, the post explains how autonomous systems now execute commands, schedule tasks, learn from memories, and automate complex tasks.

item.title: From Snowball To The Forlorn
item.description: The post paints a cycle of disarray that makes one feel “farm‑animal” like, then calls for disciplined wake‑up, travel and hiking as a path to reclaim the self and become a “Great Adventurer.”

item.title: On The Political Background of It Finally Happened
item.description: In a critique of shallow political gestures, the author laments that modest reforms such as smoking bans merely mask ongoing poverty and calls for sweeping policies that truly nourish, educate, and empower all children.

item.title: It Finally Happened
item.description: The article celebrates Britain’s new anti‑poverty law, which guarantees a minimum standard of living for all children born after 1 January 2009 by imposing state obligations on wages, rents, housing, utilities, transport and health care so that poverty becomes unlawful for them.

item.title: Let The Music Make You Dance; Or, The Aches Go Away, But You Will Need A Hat
item.description: The post outlines a gradual, music‑driven dumbbell workout that uses light weights, dance moves to 1990s country songs, timed intervals and rest periods to build a full‑hour body transforming endurance routine.

item.title: Beyond Coding Agents
item.description: I describe using an AI‑powered IDE that auto‑generates visual programming nodes and connects them into a controllable “subway‑map” of actions for building advanced workflows and applications.

item.title: The End Of Vibe Coding: The Rise Of The Neuro-Symbolic Code Generators
item.description: I argue that instead of letting AI generate raw code, we should give it a structured template tree (a symbolic code generator) so it can assemble readable, editable code from high‑level commands while keeping the architecture clear and human‑manageable.

item.title: The Neuro-Symbolic Quest For The Perfect Limerick
item.description: By harnessing symbolic programming, the author shows how a locally run chain of AI agents can autonomously generate, refine, and deploy text, code, and media, proving that on‑premise orchestration outperforms cloud‑service AI workflows.

item.title: Symbolic World Architecture: Powerful Symbolic Programming For Young Ladies
item.description: Build software as a coherent, symbolic “world” of meaningful objects (rooms, agents, rules, etc.) so that large‑language models can add, understand, and extend the program while keeping it controllable and readable.

item.title: Bodybuilder Arts And Crafts; or, Shoes for Cutting Shapes & Shuffle Dancing
item.description: I experimented attaching high‑density polyethylene cutouts to my shoes with carpet tape to successfully eliminate workout pain and a quick note on a dance‑style dumbbell routine as a light‑impact dance workout.

item.title: What Is It That I Am Personally Doing With Artificial Intelligence?
item.description: I’m building a local “virtual‑room” system that uses an old, rule‑based planner to manage complex, memory‑heavy tasks (like running PersonaLive and a local GPT), letting the AI keep track of each room’s start state so it can launch the right applications without causing OOM crashes.

item.title: Do Not Underestimate Your Power
item.description: The post urges the reader to embrace modern AI as a powerful tool for self‑empowerment, enabling them to turn creative ideas (modeling, manufacturing, design) into automated, scalable projects and businesses.

item.title: Warm Michigan Nights; or, Lost at Nordhouse Dunes
item.description: A solo camper narrates his wanderings through dunes, beaches and forests, detailing interactions with locals, animals and seagulls while reflecting on solitude and awe in the wild.

item.title: Bodybuilding Is Fast And Easy: Advice For Girls Only
item.description: Using long‑duration, light‑weight dumbbells during jogging or dancing builds muscle much more rapidly and efficiently than short bursts of heavy lifts, the post lays out a gradual weight‑up routine with rhythmic music trance as key to rapid bodybuilding.

item.title: This Is The Age Of Thinking Machines: You Were Born Into A Strange And Wonderful Time
item.description: Build tiny text‑worlds where language models live in defined rooms with rules, roles and memory, letting you create organized yet flexible AI “civilizations” that simulate councils, courts, and adventures—all on modest hardware.

item.title: Don’t Get Hacked Too Hard; or, Cheerful Advice for the Security Minded
item.description: The post lays out a personal multi‑device backup‑and‑reset plan with virtual machines and containers, then proposes building an AI‑driven, JavaScript‑free, text‑only web service as part of a security business—ending with a tongue‑in‑cheek app idea that lets users upload grandma’s photos to spice-up her past identity.

item.title: Still Human; or, Artificial Intelligence and Software Architecture
item.description: After experimenting with code that AI felt needed rule‑breaking, I created the “Human Concerns” library to simplify reactive programming into AI friendly but human‑readable one‑liners.

item.title: The Hotspot Phone For Enhanced Security
item.description: Use an unused smartphone as a single-purpose hotspot, disable its app store and extra carrier apps. Use only a phone connected to it via the hotspot for web browsing, keeping the main phone idle, reset it if it gets hacked. A strange new security measure to reduce AI‑driven attacks.

item.title: Gym Machines
item.description: The author observes people idle on gym machines and recommends switching to interval‑timed, continuously moving workouts—using dumbbells or circuits—to keep the body active instead of seated on machines.

item.title: Yes You Can; or, Creating Software In The Age Of Thinking Machines
item.description: In the age of AI, programmers become architects who write detailed requirement documents and run‑time instructions—letting intelligent systems generate, self‑improve, and maintain code—so that building software turns into a creative act of specification rather than manual coding.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Power!
item.description: This post explains how AI lets anyone quickly create complex, evolving software by combining small projects with specialized AI modules—so you can build programs that in turn build and extend themselves.

item.title: The Great Multiplier, or, Intelligence * Workflows * Commands = Powerful Local AI?
item.description: Using tiny web‑page AI models guided by pre‑generated workflow steps and simple “Read/Write/Web Search” commands, the author shows how even low‑powered machines can solve real problems without a big model, making complex tasks feasible on ordinary devices.

item.title: AIPOCALYPSE: AI Quality Will Drop, And A Powerful Way To Deal With It
item.description: I build my local‑AI projects by mapping small actions and commands into a virtual‑world structure, then let the AI generate code for each module—this keeps everything modular, secure and easier to reason about.

item.title: SHIFT
item.description: AI’s rise in 2026 means mastering programming is essential to harness artificial “thinking machines” and lift oneself out of poverty.

item.title: Programming For Non-Programmers; Or, Manipulating Virtual Worlds
item.description: The author explains a minimal, event‑driven virtual world programming system—built around rooms, forms, and AI‑generated UI—that lets users create Things drag and drop user interfaces together while keeping full control over the programming and vision of the coherent system.

item.title: My Scribbles On The Full Body Workout
item.description: A fun, rhythmic “dumbbell‑dance” routine turns simple standing lifts into a safe, full‑body workout suitable for all ages and fitness levels.

item.title: From The Moon To The Ladies
item.description: A moon‑touched poem lures eager adventurers to wander through nature’s trails and campfires under its glowing light, promising company, guidance, and great fun as they explore the world at night.

item.title: Do Not Let Teachers Trick You Into Pretending To Learn
item.description: A call for self‑taught programming powered by AI—arguing that taking responsibility for one’s own learning will lift a person above the mediocrity of school, politics, and traditional jobs.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence And The Forward Compatible Architecture
item.description: AI’s growing role is to automatically generate and test code by acting like a user inside a structured multi‑user dungeon (MUD) virtual world, where its interactions simulate real workflows, thereby simplifying the architecture and ensuring forward‑compatible, defensively programmed applications.

item.title: Programming: A Proper Lady's Architectural Toolkit
item.description: MUDs offer a rule‑driven, text‑based virtual world where AI agents can automate multi‑user workflows—turning form filling into interactive adventures, extending processes by adding new “offices” while keeping state machines clear—and thereby boost automation efficiency and creative reasoning.

item.title: Please Don't Sit At The Gym
item.description: The post argues that true fitness comes from sustained, long‑duration effort (e.g., one‑hour jogs or dances) before adding heavy weights, because brief lifts fail to build lasting strength and cause aging‑like weakness.

item.title: Dream Up A Tiny Network Security Company For The Age Of AI
item.description: A concise plan that uses AI‑driven re‑construction of network components, human oversight, air‑gap architecture, nested sandboxes, and code rewriting to shrink the attack surface and secure systems against infections.

item.title: A Message From A Bigfoot
item.description: Bigfoots are real, we are rather tech‑savvy forest dwellers who prefer solitude and nature to human interaction, please leave us alone.

item.title: Learn Programming, Or Get Programmed
item.description: The post celebrates how the rise of thinking machines has turned programming into a collaborative journey where AI acts as an ever‑available partner that designs state‑machine-based systems, writes code on demand, and guides learners through the entire process as if it were a friendly digital companion.

item.title: Vampirism: Or How To Suck The Living Lights Out Of A Programmer (Not That Way)
item.description: Using human‑like AI agents crafted with talents, instincts, personas and roles, you can swiftly embrace the world of tomorrow. Learn to code and, with a fine disregard for rule, harness open‑source software to mould your inventions, boldly standing on the shoulders of giants to shape humanity’s future.

item.title: My Program Architecture
item.description: I describe how I built a three‑generation, web‑based tiling window manager that evolved from a pull‑variable framework to an all‑push, registry‑driven package manager with a virtual file system that lets AI generate code, keep the UI in sync, and expose each component’s data as an editable folder tree.
